operating_state: Caterpillar reported 2025 sales and revenues of $67.589 billion, representing 4% growth driven by higher equipment sales volume partially offset by unfavorable price realization. Power & Energy delivered strong growth led by data center applications and oil and gas services, while Construction Industries declined on pricing pressure and Resource Industries remained flat. Order backlog surged to $51.2 billion from $30.0 billion, with significant portions extending beyond 2026, indicating extended delivery timelines across segments.
cost_structure: Operating profit margin compressed to 16.5% from 20.2% as manufacturing costs increased largely from incremental tariff impacts of $1.8 billion, with 2026 tariff exposure projected at $2.6 billion. Cost of goods sold rose to $44.8 billion from $40.2 billion, outpacing revenue growth despite operational efficiency gains. Restructuring costs increased to $445 million from $195 million, primarily related to Rail division inventory write-downs and facility optimization initiatives.
material_events: The company executed $5.190 billion in share repurchases including accelerated agreements, with $14.937 billion remaining under authorization. Issued $2.0 billion in new debt comprising ten-year and thirty-year bonds in May 2025. Board approved the $790 million acquisition of RPMGlobal Holdings Limited expected to close February 2026, and established new credit facilities totaling $11.5 billion extending through 2030.
macro_exposures: Incremental tariffs created $1.8 billion manufacturing cost headwinds in 2025 with impacts expected to reach $2.6 billion in 2026, representing the primary new macro risk. Foreign exchange sensitivity indicates a hypothetical 10% dollar weakening would adversely impact 2026 cash flow by approximately $135 million, with primary exposures across Australian dollar, Chinese yuan, Euro, Indian rupee and Mexican peso. Global operations remain subject to trade policy uncertainty and supply chain disruptions affecting customer capital expenditure decisions.
forward_outlook: Management expects 2026 sales growth around the top end of the 5-7% CAGR target supported by strong backlog and healthy end markets, with favorable price realization of approximately 2% anticipated. Capital expenditures projected at $3.5 billion with restructuring costs expected, while the company maintains focus on returning substantially all free cash flow to shareholders. The Rail division transfers from Power & Energy to Resource Industries in March 2026, requiring historical period recasting.
uncertainty_profile: Tariff mitigation actions are planned for 2026, though without these measures the impact could be approximately 20% higher than projected levels. Unrecognized tax benefits total $1.347 billion with ongoing examinations across multiple jurisdictions, while pension obligations remain underfunded. Cybersecurity threats from increasingly sophisticated attacks targeting systems and connected products create ongoing operational and data confidentiality risks.
